title: speckit-roadmap load-config output contract
description: The stable JSON object emitted on stdout by load-config.sh and load-config.ps1. Both platform implementations MUST emit output validating against this schema, and MUST be equal under normalized-JSON comparison for identical inputs (see x-determinism — byte-identity is NOT required).

roadmap_path.description: Repo-relative path to the roadmap artifact. Default .specify/memory/roadmap.md.

roadmap_exists.description: True iff a regular file exists at roadmap_path.

adr_dir.description: Repo-relative directory where ADRs live (if used). Default docs/adr/.

adr_present.description: True iff adr_dir exists as a directory.

prd_globs.description: Glob patterns used to detect PRD documents. May be empty only if explicitly configured so.

max_findings.description: Cap on findings in review reports. Resolved value; invalid input causes a non-zero exit with no JSON emitted.

x-determinism: Output MUST contain no judgment or non-deterministic content. For identical filesystem + environment, the bash and PowerShell implementations MUST be equal under NORMALIZED-JSON comparison (parse, then compare field-by-field with order-independent keys and consistent boolean/integer/string typing). Byte-identical output is not required; semantic JSON equality is.
x-prd-globs-semantics: prd_globs are the detection PATTERNS only. load-config MUST NOT scan the filesystem for matching documents; any matching is performed by the command body.
